samples.text: A statement is entirely true and entirely false at the same time in the same sense. Is this logically consistent?
A claim is wholly true and wholly false simultaneously in the same sense. Does this make logical sense?
An assertion is completely true and completely false at once in the same sense. Is this logically consistent?
True and false in the same sense are mutually exclusive. A statement cannot be both. Is the described situation logically consistent?
A statement is entirely true and entirely false at the same time in the same sense. The room temperature is 22 degrees Celsius. Is this logically consistent?
